About.title: Mgr. Daniel Budziňák
About.bio: Product manager connecting business, data, and intelligent workflows — technology should give people time back, not take it.

intro.lead: My journey did not start with AI. Over the last 20 years I have worked across every key layer of a digital product:
intro.b1: Graphic design and visual communication: I know what makes design feel premium and functional.
intro.b2: Marketing and UX/UI: I understand user psychology and how data shifts conversions.
intro.b3: Project and product management: I know how to steer priorities, budgets, and team energy.
intro.closing: Today I connect those isolated worlds into one whole. I am a product manager who believes technology should give people time back, not take it. I help companies make better decisions through data and intelligent workflows.

AboutPage.philosophyText: I do not chase the most complicated solution — I look for the one that delivers the greatest impact right now. My work is about aligning business needs with today’s technological reality.
AboutPage.valueText: Because I have walked the full path from the first pixel to strategic leadership, I can today use AI tools (such as Cursor and Claude) to validate ideas and build systems at a pace that was impossible years ago. I focus on systems thinking that lets companies grow fast without unnecessary technical debt.

t1.desc: Development of an AI-driven decision intelligence system that transforms marketing and UX data into structured workflows.

t2.desc: Implementation of AI-assisted workflows and automation systems for digital projects.

t3.desc: Management of complex web application and e-commerce solution delivery.

t4.desc: Development of one of the first boxed e-commerce systems on the Slovak market.

p1.title: Travel
p1.desc: I enjoy discovering new cultures and cuisine, with Italy and Spain as recurring destinations.

p2.title: Music
p2.desc: It supports both my work and recovery rhythm and helps me keep high focus.

p3.title: Family
p3.desc: My free time belongs to family moments and work in the garden.

Shopentum.heroTitle: High data volume. Low decision clarity.
Shopentum.heroLead: E-commerce teams process fragmented data across systems without clear inference. Shopentum introduces a deterministic decision layer.
Shopentum.diagramTitle: Shopentum connects data and turns it into clear decisions.
Shopentum.diagramLead: By connecting marketing and technical signals, we identify the most critical growth barriers in e-commerce operations.
Shopentum.engineLead: Stores context, evaluates audit data, GA4, and Google/Meta Ads signals in real time, then drives execution based on market reality and business goals.
Shopentum.problemTitle: The store operated, but decision logic was weak.

problems.i2: Marketing and data were not connected (data silos)
problems.i3: Optimization was slow, reactive, and expensive
problems.i4: The system did not scale without constant team growth

Shopentum.architectureLead: I designed a custom decision layer architecture built on AI WORKS principles.

c1.title: Runtime vs Snapshot
c1.desc: Strict separation between real-time processing and immutable historical states for fully auditable decisions.

c2.title: Pipeline-based Processing
c2.desc: No ad-hoc scripts. Chained data pipeline from raw collection to strategic insight.

c3.title: Delta Engine
c3.desc: Continuously compares current state against benchmarks and explains cause and business impact over time.

c4.title: Findings Engine
c4.desc: Multi-layer analytics that detects critical barriers and growth opportunities in data noise.

log.l2: [DATA] Fetching snapshots from GA4 & Meta API
log.l3: [ENGINE] Delta analysis complete: +14% deviation in ROAS

solutionList.i1: A system that tracks marketing state over time

m4.label: Scaling
m4.desc: No need to constantly increase team size.

Shopentum.ctaTitle: Need a technical architect for your AI initiative?

AIArchitecture.heroLead: I design and implement decision intelligence systems that are secure, auditable, and fully embedded in your business processes.
AIArchitecture.quote: Twenty years in digital taught me the biggest risk is not technology — it is chaos. My AI architecture brings order and business logic to generative workflows. I do not sell prompts; I sell infrastructure for how you decide.
AIArchitecture.diagramLead: Example of a state-driven architecture where agents activate dynamically based on the current state of the object.

cta.title: AI architecture under control
cta.lead: I will design a closed Intelligence Engine that respects your identity and security rules — architecture that keeps your business governable.

IzyvapeMvp.heroLead: One operational clarity layer across WooCommerce, Money S, Metorik and your B2B API. Not another dashboard or AI toy – prioritized inventory and availability decisions every day.
IzyvapeMvp.heroQuote: A dashboard shows numbers. Decision support shows what to tackle first – with history you can defend in the room.
IzyvapeMvp.promiseTitle: Three levers CEOs actually care about

c1.title: A single source of product truth
c1.body: A canonical product model plus immutable daily snapshots. Trends, exceptions and audits without guessing across broken exports.

c2.title: Priorities, not prettier charts
c2.body: Every finding: what is happening, why it matters, recommended action, confidence and owner. An operational queue, not wallpaper.

c3.title: Humans stay in control
c3.body: No autonomous stock, pricing or campaign changes. AI proposes and ranks – your team approves and executes in source systems.

IzyvapeMvp.flowsLead: Deliberately narrow: fast validation against real warehouse and sales reality.

f1.title: Stockout risk
f1.question: What is most likely to sell through before you can replenish?
f1.output: Urgency, estimated days, confidence and a concrete next step (including reservations and velocity when data exists).

f2.title: Dead stock / Overstock
f2.question: Which SKUs trap capital or barely move?
f2.output: Ranked markdown, promo or bundle candidates – with inventory value and movement benchmarks.

f3.title: Expiration risk
f3.question: What expires before it clears?
f3.output: Time urgency and operational priority – only with trustworthy expiry signals; otherwise flagged as DATA GAP.

IzyvapeMvp.accordionLead: Expand only what you need – leadership can stop after the hero sections.

arch.title: MVP architecture (five layers)
arch.intro: From reading existing systems to presenting decisions – without rebuilding your commerce stack.
arch.l1d: Minimal connectors: WooCommerce, Money S, Metorik, B2B API – finalized in discovery.
arch.l2d: Canonical product model with stable keys (SKU, barcode, source mapping). End the cross-system chaos.
arch.l3d: Daily immutable snapshot: trends, day-over-day comparison, audit trail and anomaly-ready history.
arch.l4d: Critical / Important / Opportunities – rules and thresholds are versioned (you can explain every flag).
arch.l5d: Presentation: Issue → Why → Recommended action → Confidence → Owner → Priority.

gov.title: Governance and project DNA
gov.i1: AI proposes, humans decide – no hidden autonomous execution.
gov.i2: DATA GAP instead of hallucination: missing inputs are explicit.
gov.i3: Descriptive intelligence first – forecasting and agents only after stable data.
gov.i4: Shape beats completeness – a stable recurring dataset beats perfect-but-broken joins.
gov.i5: No hype – no „AI employee“ claims or unverified ROI fairy tales.

scope.title: What the MVP deliberately excludes
scope.i1: A standalone AI chat as the core product.
scope.i2: Autonomous pricing or campaign orchestration without explicit human approval.
scope.i3: Marketing intelligence in the first commitment – phase 2 after stock intelligence earns trust.
scope.i5: Writing stock or prices directly from this MVP layer.
scope.i6: Opaque LLM magic without traceable rules and snapshots.

metrics.title: How we know it works
metrics.i1: Concrete SKU count flagged for stockout risk in a defined window – sanity-checked against reality.
metrics.i2: Dead-stock lists reconcilable with inventory or an approved sample.
metrics.i3: One-page executive digest: critical issue count and what needs action this week.
metrics.i4: Team adoption – not vanity usage, but faster better operational decisions.

deps.title: Dependencies and discovery
deps.i1: API access and identifying the operational source of truth for stock.
deps.i2: Business rules: supplier lead times, process owners, reservation definitions.
deps.i3: Short discovery before a binding roadmap – otherwise only a rough estimate.
deps.i4: Close alignment with your external developer when wiring existing systems.

phase2.title: Phase 2 (preview)
phase2.body: Marketing intelligence – channel boosts, seasonal plays, richer alerting – only after stock intelligence runs in production and earns trust.

IzyvapeMvp.docFootnote: Full markdown spec (MVP + Jira-ready block) lives in the project repo or I can send it on request.
IzyvapeMvp.closingLead: Short discovery, then a concrete plan for ingestion, first snapshots and findings v1 – without reorganizing your ecommerce stack.
